stage2.narrative:
In 2015, an internal investigation into the Everett School District's Special Services Department resulted in nearly $55,000 in legal fees for the district. The investigation was launched following complaints that students were not receiving services they are entitled to under the law.

In an undated report, a family filed a $20 million claim against the Everett School District, alleging that a student was subjected to sustained bullying and physical assault. The family alleges that the district permitted an environment in which such conduct was able to occur.

In 2023, reporting indicated that a state oversight body took a limited role when presented with concerns about an organization called Northwest SOIL, including a 2021 allegation that a staffer had committed emotional and physical abuse against a student enrolled in the Everett School District. The state's response to those concerns was described as hands-off.
